content: Just as human language learners use deliberate written reflection (taught in high English language curricula under the reflexion vocabulary framework) to refine writing craft by confronting inconsistencies between intended meaning and actual output, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by incorporating structured, graded reflexion exercises modeled on second-language writing workshops. This approach frames LLM self-reflection not as a novel one-off AI trick, but as a iterated skill-building practice that mirrors how humans learn to correct and improve their own output.
